metadata.scope_note: Nigeria v1 sector overlay pack. Use together with core_control_library for baseline privacy, cybersecurity, governance, evidence, and human-review controls.
metadata.versioning_note: This is a frozen starter snapshot. Do not mutate after use in reports; publish v2025_02 or later for changes.

official_sources_consulted.name: NCC Consumer Code of Practice Regulations 2024
NCC Data Protection Communications Services materials
NCC Internet Code of Practice materials

limitations: Rules encode document-evidence checks, not a final legal determination.
Each rule requires validation against current Nigerian laws, regulations, regulator circulars, directives, codes and guidance before production reliance.

rules.title: NCC licence, permit, or authorization is evidenced
rules.summary: Telecom documents should evidence NCC licence, permit, class licence, spectrum assignment, numbering authorization, or regulatory approval.

required_signals: licensed by the Nigerian Communications Commission

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Consumer code, complaints, and customer-care process is documented
rules.summary: Telecom customer-facing documents should evidence consumer-code compliance, complaints, customer care, dispute handling, or quality-of-service escalation.

source_basis: NCC Consumer Code of Practice Regulations 2024
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Subscriber information privacy and data protection controls are documented
rules.summary: Telecom documents should evidence protection of subscriber information, privacy policy, consent, personal data, or data-protection controls.

source_basis: NCC Consumer Code of Practice Regulations 2024
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Network/security incident controls are documented
rules.summary: Telecom documents should evidence network security, incident response, access control, cybersecurity, abuse management, or continuity controls.

source_basis: NCC Internet Code of Practice materials
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Critical communications infrastructure protection is addressed where applicable
rules.summary: Telecom infrastructure documents should evidence critical infrastructure protection, CNII, network resilience, audit, or protection plans where applicable.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: SIM/subscriber registration controls are referenced where applicable
rules.summary: Telecom documents should evidence subscriber registration, SIM registration, NIN linkage, KYC, or verification controls where applicable.

source_basis: NCC subscriber registration/NIN linkage regulatory materials
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Service-quality, outage, or fault management is documented
rules.summary: Telecom operational documents should evidence quality-of-service monitoring, outage handling, fault management, availability, or service restoration.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Internet access / online safety obligations are documented where applicable
rules.summary: Internet service or platform-facing telecom documents should evidence cyber safety, online harm, abuse reporting, takedown, or user-safety process.

source_basis: NCC Internet Code of Practice materials
NITDA Code of Practice for Internet Intermediaries
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Corporate and regulated operating identity is present
rules.summary: Telecom documents should reference corporate registration, operating entity name, RC number, service category, or NCC identifier.

source_basis: NCC licensing and corporate registration evidence expectations
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Data retention, disclosure, or lawful request handling is documented
rules.summary: Telecom documents should evidence retention, lawful disclosure, law-enforcement request handling, confidentiality, or access controls.

source_basis: NCC Consumer Code of Practice Regulations 2024
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.
